message:
net/websocket: make send/recv/flush cancel-correct under Cx cancellation

Thread Cx cancellation into every pollable IO path in the WebSocket
client, server, and split implementations so an in-flight send, recv,
or flush surfaces `io::ErrorKind::Interrupted` promptly when the
owning Cx is cancelled, instead of blocking indefinitely on the
underlying IO future.

Before this change, `WebSocket::send` / `WebSocket::recv` only checked
Cx at entry via `cx.checkpoint()`, so a cancel issued while a
`poll_read` / `poll_write` / `poll_flush` was already pending never
woke the task until the IO itself made progress. That left cancel-on-
close scenarios relying on the remote peer's behavior to unblock the
cancellation, which is the exact opposite of what "cancel-correct"
means in the Cx cancellation model.

src/net/websocket/client.rs:

- `send` now inspects the error from `send_frame_with_entropy` and,
  when it sees `Interrupted` with `cx.is_cancel_requested()`, runs
  `initiate_close(CloseReason::going_away())` under a
  `close_handshake.close_timeout()` bound before propagating the
  Interrupted error. This guarantees the peer sees a clean
  going-away close frame on cancel rather than a half-open socket,
  and the close attempt itself is wrapped in `time::timeout` so a
  dead peer cannot stall the cancellation indefinitely.
- `recv` now treats an `Interrupted` from `read_more()` as a
  continue-the-loop signal when `cx.is_cancel_requested()`, so the
  outer recv loop re-checks cancel state on its next iteration
  instead of bubbling Interrupted out as a protocol error.
- `flush_write_buf`, `write_buf_inline`, and the shared
  `read_some_io` helper all wrap their `poll_fn` closures in a
  `Cx::current().is_some_and(|c| c.is_cancel_requested())` guard
  that short-circuits with a synthesized
  `io::Error::new(Interrupted, "cancelled")` before touching the
  underlying IO, so a cancel issued mid-write is observed on the
  next poll instead of waiting for writable-readiness.

src/net/websocket/server.rs: mirror the same cancellation plumbing
for the server-side `send`/`recv`/`flush_write_buf`/`write_buf_inline`/
`read_some_io` so the client and server paths cannot drift. The
server send path also runs `initiate_close(going_away)` under
`close_timeout` before surfacing Interrupted so a cancelled server
handler closes the frame cleanly.

src/net/websocket/split.rs: apply the same cancel guard inside
`flush_shared_write_buf_with_permit`'s `poll_fn` closure so split
write halves honor cancellation even when a permit-holding task is
mid-flush. Without this guard, a cancelled split-writer could hold
the write permit through a blocked `poll_write` and starve the
rest of the pipeline.

tests/test_ws_cancel.rs: new integration-style test
`test_recv_wakes_on_cancellation` that builds a `WebSocket` on a
`TestIo` with zero data, spawns a child task that calls
`cx_clone.cancel_fast(CancelKind::User)` after a 100ms sleep, and
asserts the outer `recv(&cx)` call returns `Err(Interrupted)` and
transitions the connection to `CloseState::CloseSent` inside the
wall-clock window. This is the negative test the runtime was
missing: before this commit it hung indefinitely because recv
only observed cancel at entry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
